source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Well C-11, SM Block 281C

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, South Marsh Island Block 281

event.top_event: Structural damage to wellhead and platform with uncontrolled hydrocarbon gas release
actions_taken: Personnel evacuated to Lift Boat New Orleans
Casing beams and casing jack rigged up
Well C-11 tree pulled up 3 inches with casing jack
5,000 psi ball valve installed on 9.625-inch production casing
500-foot cement balance plug set from 11,100 feet to 10,600 feet
Permanent well abandonment completed on 29 September 2023
event.summary: On 9 September 2023 at 1630 hours, while Apache contractors were lifting 11.875-inch casing from Well C-28 during abandonment operations on SM Block 281C platform, the 13.375-inch surface casing on adjacent Well C-11 collapsed approximately 3 feet due to severe corrosion of the 26-inch conductor casing. The collapse caused partial shearing of the 9.625-inch production casing valve, releasing 704 psi of gas lift pressure into the atmosphere. Platform decking and wellhead safety systems were damaged. No injuries or pollution occurred. Apache failed to immediately notify BSEE as required by regulation.
recommendations: BSEE Lafayette District made no recommendations to the Office of Incident Investigations

hazards.name: Loss of conductor casing structural integrity
Trapped gas lift pressure in annulus

threats.name: 26-inch conductor casing complete metal loss due to corrosion
13.375-inch surface casing exposure to oxygen and moisture
13.375-inch surface casing wall loss and structural failure
Casing collapse onto wellhead and platform structure
Valve shearing due to casing movement

consequences.name: Uncontrolled release of hydrocarbon gas to atmosphere
Structural damage to platform decking and grating

controls.name: Monthly casing pressure monitoring and reporting
controls.barrier_role: Detection of pressure anomalies indicating casing integrity issues
controls.lod_basis: Routine monitoring to detect early signs of casing degradation

supporting_text: BSEE reviewed the last monthly casing report for Well C-11 conducted on 21 August 2023, that stated the A-Annulus current pressure was 0 psi

controls.name: Casing diagnostics and integrity assessment
controls.barrier_role: Identification of corrosion and structural degradation
controls.lod_basis: Technical inspection to assess casing condition and remaining wall thickness

supporting_text: BSEE gathered all available incident-related documents including Apache Well C-11 Casing Diagnostics Report
The 26-inch conductor casing was severely corroded, and complete metal loss was observed

controls.name: Wellhead valve design and installation
controls.barrier_role: Containment of gas pressure and prevention of uncontrolled release
controls.lod_basis: Physical barrier to contain pressurized fluids

supporting_text: the valve for the 13.375-inch casing was completely sheared off of the well and the valve for the 9.625-inch production casing was partially sheared off leaking gas from the well
The A-Annulus casing valve for the 9.625-inch production casing was partially sheared off from the wellhead and gas lift pressure was released into the atmosphere

controls.name: Platform structural design and support
controls.barrier_role: Structural containment and support of wellhead equipment
controls.lod_basis: Physical structure designed to support and contain wellhead loads

supporting_text: The metal grating and decking around the Well C-11 was damaged when the well fell
The wellhead and the platform structure around Well C-11 was damaged during this incident

controls.name: Immediate notification to BSEE of structural damage and hydrocarbon release
controls.barrier_role: Regulatory compliance and emergency response coordination

supporting_text: Apache failed to notify BSEE as per 30CFR 250.188 and NTL 2019-NO5 of an incident that resulted in structural damage to the platform, safety systems, and causing an uncontrolled release of hydrocarbons into the atmosphere
Apache Corporation failed to immediately notify the BSEE Lafayette District Manager of an incident that occurred on 9 September 2023

controls.name: Emergency response and personnel evacuation procedures
controls.barrier_role: Protection of personnel from hazardous conditions
controls.lod_basis: Emergency procedures to protect personnel during incident

supporting_text: the contract crew felt the production platform shake and heard a loud noise coming from the well bay area. The contract crew evacuated from the production platform onto the Lift Boat New Orleans
All well operations were stopped, and the well abandonment contractors evacuated to the Lift Boat New Orleans

controls.name: Stop Work Authority implementation
controls.barrier_role: Halt of operations to prevent further damage or escalation
controls.lod_basis: Management control to stop ongoing operations during incident

supporting_text: The Co-Rep called a Stop Work Authority with all personnel on the lift boat

controls.name: Specialist wellhead services mobilization and repair
controls.barrier_role: Restoration of containment and pressure control
controls.lod_basis: Technical intervention to restore wellhead integrity and containment

supporting_text: contacted B&B Wellhead Services (B&B) to have a technician mobilized to the SM-281C location
The B&B Technician installed a 5,000 pounds per square inch (psi) ball valve on the 9.625-inch production casing

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
